claims_verified.claim: Launcher runs exactly one `--phase new` invocation, no clarify/design/loop
claims_verified.evidence: create-spec.sh:58 single call; test_runs_only_phase_new_exactly_once and static text-guard test both pass
create-spec.sh:35-36 hard die; poison env.vps test still exits 0 via env.local

claims_verified.claim: Exit code propagation and hint suppression on refusal
claims_verified.evidence: set -euo pipefail + no trailing exit code override at create-spec.sh:58; bash exits on first failing command under errexit, confirmed by test_refusal_propagates_and_suppresses_hint

claims_verified.claim: env_guard tracked-file check behaves correctly even in non-git test fixtures
claims_verified.evidence: env_guard.sh treats a non-git dir / untracked file identically (quiet path); real ENGINE_DIR is a git repo with env.local gitignored, so ls-files returns nonzero and proceeds

claims_verified.claim: "draft-proposal" and its refusal semantics named in the new prompt match actual code
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/spec.py:30 DRAFT_STATUS = "draft-proposal"; run.py:218 spec.is_draft gate matches prompt's claim

claims_verified.claim: Local-author -> hub -> VPS kickoff handoff is architecturally sound (Notes-level risk, not a defect)
claims_verified.evidence: gitops.py:79-127 task_worktree bases a fresh worktree off origin/<branch> when the remote branch already exists (as create-spec's push creates); traced end to end, no code path breaks it

ran independently: ruff clean; basedpyright 0 errors (only pre-existing unrelated warnings); tests/test_create_spec_launcher.py + tests/test_spec_author_prompt.py 14 passed; tests/test_run_cli.py 98 passed

test_assessment: All 8 acceptance criteria have a directly corresponding test, independently re-run and passing. Failure-mode coverage is adequate for a thin, control-flow-free launcher: every validation/missing-env path asserts python is never reached, the single-phase invariant is checked both dynamically (stub recording argv) and statically (source-text scan excluding comments), and exit-code propagation plus hint suppression on a simulated _phase_new refusal is directly tested. No new control flow was introduced in _phase_new itself (diff is prompt text only), so its existing test coverage (test_run_cli.py, 98 passed) still applies unchanged.
residual_risks: The local-author -> hub -> VPS kickoff handoff is architecturally verified by reading gitops.py but not exercised by an automated end-to-end test, per the spec's own Notes section.
Extra positional args after <task> are silently ignored rather than rejected; harmless but could mask a typo'd invocation.
